The diseased parts of the knee are replaced with metal and plastic. Approximately 90% of patients who have total knee replacement experience a significant reduction in pain and a Advance Care dramatic improvement in their ability to perform common activities of daily living. High Healthcare Proxy: No impact activities should be avoided after surgery, if possible, to prolong the life of the Living Will: No implant. Serious complications occur in less than 2% of patients. Diagnoses are listed in the order that best reflects the seriousness of each condition and supports the disciplines and services provided. Rate the degree of symptom control for each condition in Column 2. ICD-10-CM sequencing requirements must be followed if multiple coding is indicated for any diagnoses. Code each row according to the following directions for each column: Column 1: Enter the description of the diagnosis. Sequencing of diagnoses should reflect the seriousness of each condition and support the disciplines and services provided. Column 2: Enter the ICD-10-CM code for the condition described in Column 1 no surgical or procedure codes allowed. Codes must be entered at the level of highest specificity and ICD-10-CM coding rules and sequencing requirements must be followed. Note that external cause codes (ICD-10-CM codes beginning with V, W, X, or Y) may not be reported in M1021 (Primary Diagnosis) but may be reported in M1023 (Secondary Diagnoses). Also note that when a Z-code is reported in Column 2 : the code for the underlying condition can often be entered in Column 2, as long as it is an active on-going condition impacting home health care. Rate the degree of symptom control for the condition listed in Column 1. Do not assign a symptom control rating if the diagnosis code is a V, W, X, Y or Z-code. Choose one value that represents the degree of symptom control appropriate for each diagnosis using the following scale: 0 - Asymptomatic, no treatment needed at this time 1 - Symptoms well controlled with current therapy 2 - Symptoms controlled with difficulty, affecting daily functioning; patient needs ongoing monitoring 3 - Symptoms poorly controlled; patient needs frequent adjustment in treatment and dose monitoring 4 - Symptoms poorly controlled; history of re-hospitalizations Note that the rating for symptom control in Column 2 should not be used to determine the sequencing of the diagnoses listed in Column 1. These are separate items and sequencing may not coincide.Insert Diagnosis PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Clinical Group: MS_REHAB)SeverityClear Selection 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 1 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: No_group)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 2 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: Endocrine_3)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 3 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: No_group)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 4 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription (Comorbidity Group: Behavioral_2)SeverityClear SelectionSet as Primary 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbate OTHER DIAGNOSIS - 5 ICD-10 CodeOnset DateDescription SeverityClear Selection 0 1 2 3 4 ^OnsetExacerbateInsert Diagnosis(M1028) Active Diagnoses:Comorbidities and Co-existing Conditions Check all that apply See OASIS Guidance Manual for a complete list of relevant ICD-10 codes.1. Peripheral Vascular Disease (PVD) or Peripheral Arterial Disease (PAD) 2. Diabetes Mellitus (DM) 3. If You Did Not Receive ALL Pages, Please Call Our Office
8402 West Centennial Parkway 8205 West Warm Springs Road 2930 West Horizon Ridge Parkway
ICD 10: S/P 1 L.TKA skilled nursing visit for
3033 Harewood Ave N Las Vegas, NV
2011 E Lake Mead Blvd North Las Vegas,
F/U Knee Osteoarthritis, Left evaluated on March 25, 2026
HPI: This is a 66 year old female who:
1. is following up for Knee Osteoarthritis on the left knee.
She is here to discuss surgery.
Left Knee: Normal alignment, no deformity, no
The following images were ordered and obtained:
Additional Images: LT KNEE 3V WB AP, LAT, SUNRISE
Specific Findings: xrays demonstrating severe degenerative joint disease with joint space
Please refer to the education handout for detailed counseling.
The importance of maintaining an ideal body weight or
losing weight to reduce stress on the knee joint is a cornestone of nonoperative
The diseased parts of the knee are replaced with metal and plastic.
dramatic improvement in their ability to perform common activities of daily living.
impact activities should be avoided after surgery, if possible, to prolong the life of the
Serious complications occur in less than 2% of patients.
limited to infection, injury to blood vessels and nerves, blood clots, heart complications,
Other Instructions: Post-op visit 10-14 dates after surgery date
physical therapy, and use of supportive
Electronically Signed By: Arjun Patel, DO, 04/03/2026 08:07 AM PDT
